steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $4{t}^{2}s \left(18{s}^{2}-15t\right)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content:
Find the GCF of $72t^2s^3$ and $60t^3s$. The numerical GCF of 72 and 60 is 12; the variable GCF is $t^2s$ (lowest power of $t$ is 2, lowest power of $s$ is 1). The combined GCF is $12t^2s$, but the answer choices factor only $4t^2s$ or $4ts$ out, so pull out $4t^2s$:
$$72t^2s^3 - 60t^3s = 4t^2s(18s^2 - 15t)$$

steps.title: Test each choice by distributing
steps.content:
• $4ts \left(18{s}^{2}-15ts\right)$: $4ts(18s^2 - 15ts) = 72ts^3 - 60t^2s^2$ — wrong exponents on $t$.
  • $4ts \left(18{t}^{2}{s}^{2}-15t\right)$: $4ts(18t^2s^2 - 15t) = 72t^3s^3 - 60t^2s$ — wrong exponents.
  • $4{t}^{2}s \left(18ts-15s\right)$: $4t^2s(18ts - 15s) = 72t^3s^2 - 60t^2s^2$ — wrong exponents on $s$.
  • $4{t}^{2}s \left(18{s}^{2}-15t\right)$: $4t^2s(18s^2 - 15t) = 72t^2s^3 - 60t^3s$ — matches the original.
That matches the correct answer, so D is correct.
